---
title: "R Tip of the Day: ggiraph"
author: "Mary Phillips"
format:
  revealjs: 
    theme: solarized
    transition: fade
    slide-number: true
    chalkboard: 
      buttons: true
    preview-links: auto
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r}
#|include: FALSE
library(ggiraph)
library(tidyverse)
library(nycflights13)
library(patchwork)

flights <- nycflights13::flights 
flights_jan1 <- flights |>
  filter(origin == "EWR" & month == 1 & day == 1 & hour == 13) |>
  mutate(name = str_glue("EWR to {dest}, ",
                         "flight num {flight}"))

dinos <- tribble(
  ~type, ~year, ~perc, ~unique,
  "love", 2024, 13, "love 2024",
  "like", 2024, 31, "like 2024",
  "neither", 2024, 47, "neither 2024",
  "dislike", 2024, 2, "dislike 2024",
  "hate", 2024, 3, "hate 2024",
  "don't know", 2024, 4, "don't know 2024",
  "love", 2026, 17, "love 2026",
  "like", 2026, 30, "like 2026",
  "neither", 2026, 47, "neither 2026",
  "dislike", 2026, 2, "dislike 2026",
  "hate", 2026, 1, "hate 2026",
  "don't know", 2026, 3, "don't know 2026"
)
dinos$type <- fct_inorder(dinos$type)
```

## What is ggiraph?

::: {.fragment .fade-in}
- ![](https://github.com/philli17/R_Tip_of_the_Day/blob/main/ggiraph_hex.png?raw=true)

- [ggiraph is an extension of the ggplot package that allows you to make interactive graphs ]{style="color:darkorange1;"}
:::

::: {.fragment .fade-in}
- It is designed specifically to work with ggplot, so it works well with RMarkdown, Quarto and Shiny.
:::

## What can ggiraph do?

::: {.fragment .fade-in}
```{r}
dinoplot <- dinos |>
  ggplot() +
  geom_point_interactive(aes(x = type,
             y = perc,
             color = as.factor(year),
             tooltip = unique,
             data_id = unique),
    size = 3, position = "jitter", hover_nearest = TRUE) +
  labs(title = "How much do you like dinosuars?",
       caption = "Data from YouGov's polls, Dinosaurs
       https://yougov.com/en-us/topics/topic/Dinosaurs",
       color = "Year",
       x = "How much do you Like or Dislike dinosaurs?",
       y = "Percentage of Answers")
girafe(ggobj = dinoplot)
```
:::

## Scatterplot Code {auto-animate=true}
```{.r code-line-numbers="3,4,5,6,7,9,17"}
dinoplot <- dinos |>
  ggplot() +
  geom_point_interactive(aes(x = type,
             y = perc,
             color = as.factor(year),
             tooltip = unique,
             data_id = unique),
             size = 3, 
             position = "jitter", 
             hover_nearest = TRUE) +
  labs(title = "How much do you like dinosuars?",
       caption = "Data from YouGov's polls, Dinosaurs
       https://yougov.com/en-us/topics/topic/Dinosaurs",
       color = "Year",
       x = "How much do you Like or Dislike dinosaurs?",
       y = "Percentage of Answers")
girafe(ggobj = dinoplot)
```

## Another Example: NYC Flights

::: {.fragment .fade-in}
```{r}
scatter <- ggplot(
  data = flights_jan1,
  mapping = aes(
    x = dep_delay, y = arr_delay,
    tooltip = name,
    data_id = name
  )
) +
  geom_point_interactive(
    size = 3, hover_nearest = TRUE
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Departure and Arrival Delays",
    x = "Departure Delay (minutes)", y = "Arrival Delay (minutes)"
  ) +
  theme_bw()

bar <- ggplot(
  data = flights_jan1,
  mapping = aes(
    x = reorder(flight, distance), y = distance,
    tooltip = paste(name, "<br>Dist", distance, "mi"),
    data_id = name
  )
) +
  geom_col_interactive(fill = "skyblue") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Flight Distance",
    x = "Flight Number", y = "Flight Distance in Miles"
  ) +
  theme_bw()

combined_plot <- scatter + bar +
  plot_layout(ncol = 2)

interactive_plot <- girafe(ggobj = combined_plot)
interactive_plot
```
:::
